Testamentary Capacity.rule: Testamentary capacity requires that the testator understand the nature of the act, the extent of the property, the natural objects of bounty, and the disposition being made.
Testamentary Capacity.application: Because the testator understood the property and beneficiaries, capacity existed.

Formal Requirements — Attested Will.rule: An attested will must be in writing, signed by the testator, and witnessed by at least two witnesses.
Formal Requirements — Attested Will.application: Because the will was signed and witnessed, it is valid.

Holographic Will.rule: A holographic will is valid if it is entirely in the testator’s handwriting and signed.
Holographic Will.application: Because the will was handwritten and signed, it is valid.

Codicil.rule: A codicil is an amendment to a will that must satisfy will formalities.
Codicil.application: Because the codicil met formalities, it is effective.

Revocation — By Physical Act.rule: A will may be revoked by a physical act performed with intent to revoke.
Revocation — By Physical Act.application: Because the testator destroyed the will intentionally, it was revoked.

Revocation — By Subsequent Writing.rule: A will may be revoked by a later will or codicil that expressly or impliedly revokes the prior will.
Revocation — By Subsequent Writing.application: Because the later will conflicted, the prior will was revoked.

Dependent Relative Revocation.rule: Dependent relative revocation applies when a testator revokes a will based on a mistaken belief.
Dependent Relative Revocation.application: Because the revocation was based on mistake, the prior will may be revived.

Revival of Will.rule: A revoked will may be revived if the testator intends revival.
Revival of Will.application: Because the testator intended revival, the will is revived.

Anti-Lapse Statute.rule: An anti-lapse statute saves a gift when the beneficiary predeceases the testator and is a close relative.
Anti-Lapse Statute.application: Because the beneficiary was a relative, the gift passes to descendants.

Ademption by Extinction.rule: Ademption occurs when specifically devised property is not in the estate at death.
Ademption by Extinction.application: Because the property was sold, the gift adeems.

Abatement.rule: Abatement determines the order in which gifts are reduced to satisfy debts.
Abatement.application: Because assets were insufficient, abatement applied.

Elective Share.rule: A surviving spouse may claim an elective share of the decedent’s estate.
Elective Share.application: Because the spouse elected, the statutory share applies.

Intestate Succession.rule: If a decedent dies intestate, property passes according to the state intestacy statute.
Intestate Succession.application: Because no will exists, intestate succession applies.

Pretermitted Child.rule: A pretermitted child omitted from a will may take an intestate share.
Pretermitted Child.application: Because the child was omitted, a share is granted.

Undue Influence.rule: Undue influence exists when influence overcomes the testator’s free will.
Undue Influence.application: Because domination existed, undue influence is presumed.

Trust — General Rule.rule: A trust is a fiduciary relationship in which a trustee holds legal title for the benefit of beneficiaries.
Trust — General Rule.application: Because legal title is held for beneficiaries, a trust exists.

Trust Creation Requirements.rule: A valid trust requires intent, identifiable trust property, a trustee, and identifiable beneficiaries.
Trust Creation Requirements.application: Because all elements are present, the trust is valid.

Revocable Trust.rule: A revocable trust may be amended or revoked by the settlor during life.
Revocable Trust.application: Because the settlor revoked it, the trust terminated.

Irrevocable Trust.rule: An irrevocable trust cannot be modified without beneficiary consent or court approval.
Irrevocable Trust.application: Because consent was lacking, modification is denied.

Spendthrift Trust.rule: A spendthrift trust restricts both voluntary and involuntary transfer of beneficiary interests.
Spendthrift Trust.application: Because the trust has a spendthrift clause, creditors are barred.

Trustee Duties — Loyalty.rule: A trustee owes a duty of loyalty and must act solely in the beneficiaries’ interests.
Trustee Duties — Loyalty.application: Because self-dealing occurred, the duty was breached.

Trustee Duties — Care.rule: A trustee must administer the trust with reasonable care and skill.
Trustee Duties — Care.application: Because investments were reckless, the duty was breached.

Trustee Duties — Impartiality.rule: A trustee must act impartially among beneficiaries.
Trustee Duties — Impartiality.application: Because one beneficiary was favored, the duty was breached.

Trust Modification — Cy Pres.rule: Cy pres allows modification of a charitable trust to approximate the settlor’s intent.
Trust Modification — Cy Pres.application: Because the purpose became impossible, cy pres applies.

Trust Termination.rule: A trust may terminate when its purpose is fulfilled or becomes impossible.
Trust Termination.application: Because the purpose ended, the trust terminates.

Resulting Trust.rule: A resulting trust arises when trust property is returned to the settlor or estate.
Resulting Trust.application: Because the trust failed, a resulting trust arose.

Constructive Trust.rule: A constructive trust is imposed to prevent unjust enrichment.
Constructive Trust.application: Because property was obtained by wrongdoing, a constructive trust is imposed.
